description: Version-pinned and entirely-malicious package IOCs. Shipped with repo-forensics as baseline detection. Optional OSV feed adds live updates (see --osv flag). Use '*' in the version list to mean 'any version of this package is malicious'.

chalk_debug_sep_2025.title: Chalk/Debug supply chain compromise
chalk_debug_sep_2025.notes: Maintainer credentials phished. Malicious versions published for several hours before takedown. Payload hooked crypto wallet provider APIs to redirect transactions.

duckdb_sep_2025.title: DuckDB supply chain compromise
duckdb_sep_2025.notes: Same actor as chalk/debug Sep 2025 campaign. DuckDB npm packages compromised in parallel.

eslint_prettier_phishing_jul_2025.title: ESLint/Prettier phishing compromise
eslint_prettier_phishing_jul_2025.notes: Maintainer phished. Malicious versions published for eslint-config-prettier and related packages. postinstall script exfiltrated npm tokens.

nx_s1ngularity_aug_2025.title: Nx / S1ngularity supply chain
nx_s1ngularity_aug_2025.notes: Multiple @nx/* scoped packages and the nx meta-package compromised across a range of versions. Payload scanned for GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, AWS credentials and posted to attacker webhook.

shai_hulud_worm_v1_sep_2025.title: Shai-Hulud self-propagating npm worm (v1)
shai_hulud_worm_v1_sep_2025.notes: First npm worm with self-propagation via stolen maintainer credentials. @ctrl/tinycolor was the initial vector before the worm was contained.

react_native_mar_2026.title: React Native country/phone picker compromise
react_native_mar_2026.notes: Two popular React Native packages compromised. Payload targeted mobile device credentials and keychain items.

ghost_campaign_feb_2026.title: Ghost campaign (entirely malicious packages)
ghost_campaign_feb_2026.notes: Entirely malicious packages published under ghost accounts. Names designed to look legitimate. All versions malicious.

nk_contagious_interview_wave3_mar_2026.title: North Korean Contagious Interview wave 3
nk_contagious_interview_wave3_mar_2026.notes: Third wave of DPRK-linked Contagious Interview campaign. Packages distributed to software developer targets via fake job interviews. All versions malicious.

credential_harvester_typosquats_2025.title: Credential harvester typosquats
credential_harvester_typosquats_2025.notes: Typosquat campaign targeting Discord, Ethereum, and framework package names. Often use .js suffix as typosquat signal.

nodecordrat_bitcoin_typosquats_2025.title: NodeCordRAT / Bitcoin library typosquats
nodecordrat_bitcoin_typosquats_2025.notes: Typosquats of Bitcoin library names carrying NodeCordRAT payload.

lazarus_graphalgo_may2025_feb2026.title: Lazarus Group / Graphalgo campaign
lazarus_graphalgo_may2025_feb2026.notes: Long-running Lazarus-attributed campaign using math/graph library names.

rat_typosquats_2026.title: RAT-delivering typosquats 2026
rat_typosquats_2026.notes: Typosquat packages delivering remote access trojans.
